title: Bassingham Fen Stirling Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including pilot Plt Off. Robert James Chalmers

sources.title: Geograph East Anglia WW2 Aircrew Crash Memorials

external_narratives.summary: War Memorials Online records the memorial plaque to the crew of 1660 HCU Stirling EH977 'Z', noting the aircraft crashed close to Wirelocks Farm after departing Swinderby for a routine night conversion training flight on 5 November 1944.
key_details: Crashed near Wirelocks Farm, Sand Syke Drain
Post-accident investigation found wrong engine was feathered

external_narratives.summary: Research blog entry on P/O Robert James Chalmers records he arrived at 1660 Conversion Unit on 2 October 1944, barely a month before the fatal crash. The investigation revealed that after an in-flight engine failure, a functioning engine was feathered by mistake, possibly due to crew inexperience.
key_details: Chalmers arrived at 1660 HCU on 2 October 1944

local_contacts.org_name: City of Lincoln Royal British Legion

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: War Memorials Online ref 264774 — address: Linga Lane, Bassingham Fen LN5 9LD
Roll of Honour + Geograph East Anglia WW2 memorials
location_confidence.verification_notes: SEVERE ERROR — 26.7km off. Current coords (53.2338, -0.2689) plot near Nocton/Potterhanworth, no connection to this aircraft. Actual memorial at Wirelocks Farm, Linga Lane, Bassingham Fen LN5 9LD. Freestanding stone with metal plaque erected 2014 by Lincolnshire Aircraft Recovery Group (70th anniversary). Aircraft crashed into Sand Syke Drain at Wirelocks Farm at 02:40 on 5 Nov 1944.
